title: FHIR Endpoint → CMS hospital resolution
description: Per-vendor mapping from a FHIR brands-bundle Endpoint.id to a Medicare-certified hospital row in the CMS Provider-of-Services file. Resolves the hospital-disambiguation problem flagged in tasks/hospital-disambiguation.md: the brand bundles cannot disambiguate same-named hospitals across states, and Epic publishes no address at all. This file attaches CCN, normalized address, and (where available) NPI to each endpoint so downstream consumers (fhir-studio brand_index.json, claims joins, HCRIS, Hospital Compare) can use a stable identifier.

pos_catalog.description: Relative path to the data/cms-pos/hospitals-{date}.json catalog this resolution was joined against.

matched_unique.description: Endpoints that resolved to exactly one CCN.

matched_multiple.description: Endpoints with multiple POS candidates (no auto-resolution; surfaced in candidates[]).

unmatched.description: Endpoints with no plausible POS candidate.

source_url.description: Either the CMS POS file URL or the path to the bundled catalog.

endpoint_id.description: FHIR Endpoint.id from the vendor's brands bundle. Stable per-endpoint key.

ccn.description: CMS Certification Number (PRVDR_NUM) when a unique POS row was matched. Null when unmatched or ambiguous.

npi.description: National Provider Identifier when present in the brands bundle Organization.identifier or recovered via NPPES.

name_observed.description: Endpoint.name or managingOrganization.name verbatim from the brands bundle.

name_pos.description: Hospital name from the POS catalog when matched.

candidates.description: When the resolver found multiple plausible POS rows, all are listed here. ccn is the join key.
